target: WIN-030 detail_evidence — paragraph redundancy and finding field duplicate
holes_addressed: ISS-664 paragraph redundancy — already resolved in WIN-030 detail_evidence. Paragraph 1 now makes the WGS84-projection/absorption argument once ('the ellipse is not discovering the Earth's shape — it is absorbing the radial scaling distortion that the Azimuthal Equidistant projection introduces'). Paragraph 2 adds independent information: the 48.6% figure is unreproducible from any script in the repo, the b/a ratio wanders 0.70–0.90 across V5–V9 (29% variation in a supposedly physical parameter), and OPEN-003 concedes non-convergence.
optional_enhancements_not_taken: Adding concrete AIC penalty (2k=6 for 3 params) — curmudgeon hole #3 was minor severity; current text already cites Burnham & Anderson and argues the test was never performed. Adding the 2k number without a reported log-likelihood delta would be hand-waving in the other direction.

Adding WGS84 b/a=0.99664 comparison — this is available in stronger_arguments #2 and could be a future tightening, but would re-expand paragraph 2 and risks re-introducing redundancy with paragraph 1. Leave for a cycle-2 review pass if curmudgeon re-flags.
note: Decider's suggested-patches pipeline resolved ISS-664 before analyst picked up EXP-046. Current WIN-030 detail_evidence is clean — paragraph 1 = WGS84 projection artifact argument; paragraph 2 = unreproducibility + wandering parameters + OPEN-003. No overlap. EXP-046 is a confirmation no-op.
kernel_of_truth: The dome genuinely identified a real cartographic fact: circular azimuthal equidistant projection centered on the North Pole produces systematic distance errors that grow toward the 'rim' (South Pole region). The elliptical correction is a legitimate empirical observation that the circular projection isn't working. The flaw is interpreting this as evidence of a physically elliptical disc rather than as the signature of a spherical Earth being forced onto a plane.
